author: Dr. Anna Reyes (PGY-2) / Dr. Jennifer Beckman (Medicine attending)
content:
DAILY PROGRESS NOTE — 4/11/2027, 8:30 AM (Hospital Day 56, Floor day 30)
Patient: Nguyen, Lan T (MRN-90215888)
Location: 5N bed 14
Day-before-anticipated-discharge note.
Interpreter: Vietnamese phone interpreter used.

24-HOUR EVENTS:
Quiet, stable day yesterday. Patient slept ~7 hours.
- PT yesterday afternoon: ambulated 300 feet with rolling walker, no assist needed. Climbed 6 stairs with handrail and 1-person standby — exceeded her current functional goal. Cleared for SNF transfer functionally.
- OT discharged her from inpatient OT yesterday; will continue OT services at SNF.
- Nutrition: tolerating regular diet, 90% intake. Weight today 57.4 kg (up from nadir 51.8 kg; total ICU-related loss now 4.6 kg from admission 62 kg).
- No bleeding, no fevers, no respiratory or cardiac symptoms.
- Family visited yesterday with grandchildren — patient in good spirits.

DISCHARGE DESTINATION: Confirmed today via case management — bed at FutureCare Charles Village SNF is available 4/12 (tomorrow). Transport arranged 11 AM 4/12.

SYSTEMS:

NEURO: Alert, oriented x 3 via interpreter. Mood good. Excited about discharge.

RESP: RA. SpO2 97%. No symptoms.

CV: BP 128/76. HR 76. No edema bilateral.

GI / NUTRITION: Tolerating regular diet. No symptoms. Stools normal.

RENAL: Cr 1.3 (at baseline). BUN 22. UOP good.

HEME / DVT: Right LE no swelling, no tenderness. Apixaban day 40, no bleeding events.

ID: No active issues x 6 weeks.

MSK / FUNCTIONAL: Cleared for SNF-level care by PT.

LABS (today, AM):
- CBC: WBC 6.4, Hgb 10.5, Plt 256.
- BMP: Na 138, K 3.9, Cl 102, CO2 26, BUN 22, Cr 1.3 (baseline), Glu 96.
- LFTs: AST 24, ALT 22, ALP 78, T-bili 0.5, Alb 3.2 (low normal post-ICU).
- INR 1.0.
- Magnesium 1.9, Phos 3.4, Ca 9.2.

MEDICATIONS (reconciled today, will be final reconciled tomorrow AM with pharmacy):
- Apixaban 5 mg PO BID (continue ~3 months total from 3/2; reassess 5/15 hematology)
- Lisinopril 5 mg PO daily (continue; restarted on HOLD with PCP for now — actually CORRECTION: not on hold, will continue at SNF; please see updated final med rec)
- Atorvastatin 40 mg PO daily
- Acetaminophen 1000 mg PO q6h PRN (max 3 g/day given mild Alb depression and prior LFT bumps)
- Calcium 600 mg PO daily + Vit D3 1000 IU daily
- Multivitamin PO daily
- Potassium chloride 20 mEq PO daily (continue temporary, taper at outpatient follow-up if K stable)
- Pantoprazole 40 mg PO daily (will discontinue at 4-week outpatient follow-up if no GI symptoms)

ALLERGIES: PCN, iodinated contrast (pre-medication required for future contrast studies).

DISCHARGE STATUS FROM MEDICINE PERSPECTIVE: Medically cleared for SNF transfer. Final discharge summary to be completed and signed by attending tomorrow morning prior to transport.

PROBLEMS (active, on transfer to SNF):
#1 Recovery from severe acute biliary pancreatitis with ICU complications — clinically resolved.
#2 Right LE proximal DVT — on apixaban, day 40 of treatment, asymptomatic.
#3 Cholelithiasis — known; outpatient elective cholecystectomy planning with General Surgery (Dr. Markovich) at 6/1/2027.
#4 ICU-acquired weakness — improving; rehab at SNF anticipated 4-6 weeks.
#5 CKD stage 3a, baseline (Cr 1.3, eGFR 47).
#6 HTN — well-controlled on lisinopril 5 mg.
#7 HLD — well-controlled on atorvastatin 40 mg.
#8 LTBI — known, untreated, no symptoms.
#9 Anemia (chronic + post-ICU), improving.
#10 Penicillin allergy (childhood rash, non-anaphylactic; tolerated pip/tazo this admission).
#11 Iodinated contrast allergy (urticarial; pre-medication required for future contrast).

FAMILY UPDATE: Son updated this morning via interpreter — confirmed everyone ready for transfer tomorrow.

Will round again this PM and tomorrow morning before discharge.
attestation:
Electronically signed by:
Dr. Anna Reyes, MD (PGY-2)
Dr. Jennifer Beckman, MD (Attending)
Date: 4/11/2027
